summary: New flights value-compare command runs paired cash + award searches and applies a TPG cents-per-point valuation, with a 30-day on-disk cache and a soft-fallback chain (override -> fresh cache -> live TPG fetch -> stale cache -> constant).
reason: Manual ritual of running cash search + award search + cents-per-point math + TPG lookup recurs constantly. Now one command emits an apples-to-apples comparison: cash_usd, miles + taxes_usd, effective_cpp, multiple over TPG baseline, tpg_valued_usd. A new internal/valuation/ package holds the pluggable program registry so a future amend can add United/AAdvantage/etc. by appending one struct row.
validated_outcome: End-to-end live: FCO->SEA 2026-08-30 returns cash $1626.23 (SAVER) + award 30000mi + $64.53, TPG cpp 1.4c (live-scraped), effective cpp 5.21c, multiple 3.72x, tpg_valued_usd $484.53. Replaces the previously-broken extractLowestAwardPrice (whose synthetic-test field names did not match the live SvelteKit response; award-cheapest was silently returning empty results) with a structured hydrate+walk over the actual rows[].solutions.<CABIN> shape.
